articles.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER. articles.title: Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
articles.summary: Earlier this month, I spoke at the Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit about a blind spot most security programs are still not accounting for - how attackers are circumventing AI security programs by using legacy infrastructure to hijack AI agents. articles.title: Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data
articles.summary: Vulnerable WordPress plugin iterations leak API keys, secrets, tokens, server information, and other data. The post Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: A malicious dependency the attackers added to over 140 Mastra packages fetches a payload targeting cryptocurrency extensions. The post North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Browser Bugs, EDR Killers, TV Botnet, OpenBSD Flaw, Android Trojan, and More
articles.summary: It’s Monday again. This week’s threat list looks painfully familiar: abused integrations, fake tools, poisoned websites, ransomware crews trying to shut down security tools, and mobile malware asking for way too much control. The annoying part is how little of this feels new. Weak credentials, sketc

articles.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
articles.summary: Groups like ShinyHunters are demonstrating that attackers do not necessarily need malware or zero-day exploits to cause massive damage. The post What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
articles.summary: The vulnerability exploited by the Usbliter8 exploit cannot be patched and a PoC exploit has been released by researchers. The post New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign
articles.summary: A database of over 86,000 confirmed working credentials was created during the credential-harvesting campaign. The post Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack
articles.summary: HackerOne, Huntress, Jamf, OneTrust, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium are among the affected Klue customers. The post More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
articles.summary: A new malware family is turning forgotten home routers into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network, not the DDoS botnet these devices usually end up in. articles.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
articles.summary: A new report from INTERPOL has revealed a "dramatic increase" in cybercrime in Asia and the South Pacific, fueled by rapid digitalization, internet penetration, new technologies, organized criminal networks, and a disparity in cybersecurity maturity. articles.title: Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals
articles.summary: Hackers stole personal information after breaching the systems of a third-party license vendor serving TPWD. The post Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide
articles.summary: A previously undocumented malware botnet named AryStinger has compromised more than 4,000 outdated routers to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]

articles.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
articles.summary: A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
articles.summary: Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]

articles.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites. articles.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "Icarus" extortion group publicly claims the attack. [...]

articles.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]

articles.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
articles.summary: Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published a working exploit, dubbed usbliter8, that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips. That code is burned into the silicon at manufacture. No software update can reach it. Affected devices w

articles.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing system defenses before deploying the encryptor. articles.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver’s licenses
articles.summary: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department (TPWD) disclosed a data breach at its license system vendor that exposed personal information for more than three million individuals. [...]

articles.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
articles.summary: Microsoft researchers have detailed an exploit chain, named AutoJack, that turns an AI browsing agent into a delivery vehicle for remote code execution. articles.title: Police raid malware network tied to Russia's Evil Corp hacker group
articles.summary: An international operation targeted the SocGholish botnet, which has been linked to the Russia-based cybercrime group Evil Corp.

articles.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
articles.summary: Modern phishing attacks, including Device Code phishing, can undermine MFA protections and grant attackers access to corporate accounts without stealing passwords. articles.title: From Assistive to Agentic: The AI Shift That's Redefining Threat Management
articles.summary: Introduction The average enterprise security team has 40 or more security tools, giving a lot of visibility into internal telemetry and asset data. articles.title: CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor
articles.summary: CryptoBandits uses a local SOCKS5 proxy for traffic routing, blending data theft with remote code execution. The post CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
articles.summary: The large-scale credential theft campaign hit roughly half of the internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPNs. The post FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
articles.summary: CISA has urged U.S. federal agencies to secure their systems by Sunday against a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability that is being exploited in attacks. [...]

articles.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
articles.summary: The first wave of enterprise AI concern was straightforward. It was simply employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools. articles.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: The hackers exfiltrated data from Salesforce instances of Klue customers, such as Huntress and Recorded Future. The post Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC
articles.summary: WideField will accelerate Agentic SOC capabilities by expanding the lens on threat investigation to include identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius. The post Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "FortiBleed." [...]

articles.summary: Apple has updated its Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds to patch a high-severity vulnerability that could be exploited by nearby hackers to eavesdrop on users. articles.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
articles.summary: CISA has given federal agencies only three days to patch CVE-2026-20253, which can be exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution. The post Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers to help affiliates evade detection in attacks. [...]

articles.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. articles.summary: F5 has released security updates to address two critical security flaws in NGINX Open Source that could be exploited to achieve code execution on affected systems. articles.summary: Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign codenamed CryptoBandits that has targeted users since February 2026 with clipboard-intercepting malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor anonymity network to hide communication. articles.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
articles.summary: Mackay Sugar said it was "working urgently" to verify claims that a highly active ransomware group was behind a cyberattack that shut down harvesting and milling operations.

articles.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
articles.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have charted the evolution of INC from an nascent ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation to one of the most prolific cybercrime groups in 2026, claiming no less than 830 victims since August 2023. articles.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. articles.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
articles.summary: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrime group. [...]

articles.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
articles.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack that distributed infected releases to paying customers via the vendor's official update system. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
articles.summary: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]
